sections.heading: What's a graph?
sections.body: A **graph** is just **nodes** (also called vertices) connected by **edges**. Unlike trees, there's no root and connections can form loops. Edges can point one way (**directed**) or both (**undirected**).

sections.title: Social network
sections.body: Every person is a node. Every friendship is an edge. Cities connected by roads, web pages connected by links, computers connected by cables — all graphs.

sections.heading: The two great explorations
sections.body: **BFS (Breadth-First Search)** explores level by level using a queue. **DFS (Depth-First Search)** dives deep first using a stack (or recursion). You'll use one of these to solve most graph problems.

questions.question: BFS typically uses which data structure?
questions.explanation: Queue gives BFS its level-by-level order. Stack gives DFS its depth-first behavior.
